bead.title: agent: update step-zero decomposition instructions
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Agent-facing Step 0 decomposition instructions still say the current bead cannot be closed and only allow a parent-note update during decomposition. That makes the operational answer ambiguous when an agent performs a lossless split: the decomposed bead should be completed by decomposition, and children must not depend on the parent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 forbid `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the current bead and only allow `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` during Step 0 decomposition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go contains the generated execute-bead instruction text that is rendered into prompt snapshots, so the same stale rule reaches launched agents.
- cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt and cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt lock the old prompt wording unless updated with the source instruction text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md so Step 0 says agents may file child/sibling/replacement beads, wire only child-to-child or sibling/replacement dependencies, must not add the parent as a child dependency, and may close the decomposed parent once the split is lossless and durable.
- Update cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go with the same Step 0 decomposition contract.
- Refresh cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/{agent,claude}.txt from the source prompt text and keep/update prompt guardrail tests so drift is caught.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change lifecycle code or tracker store behavior; implementation paths are separate child beads.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or generated plugin payloads under `.ddx/plugins/`.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider/model selection, or power escalation policy.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e9542e0d.
Deps: No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` state that Step 0 decomposition may close the decomposed parent after filing a lossless durable split.
2. The same files state that generated children must use `Parent=<decomposed-id>` only as hierarchy metadata and must not add the decomposed parent as a dependency; only child-to-child or sibling/replacement dependency edges may be wired.
3. `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt` match the updated prompt source.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshots|TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
